name: Matchmaking Lobby
description: A lobby gathers players into a session, assigns slots, exposes ready state, and starts a match after authority checks succeed.
design_notes: A lobby is a session state machine with race conditions: players join, leave, ready up, change teams, invite friends, and disconnect while match start is pending. The design should define lobby_id ownership, matchmaking_ticket_id lifecycle, slot assignment, host migration behavior, and the exact authority that can move the lobby into gameplay.
design_purpose: Let players form parties, inspect rules, choose teams or roles, and confirm readiness before the match begins.
Separate matchmaking constraints from in-match simulation so failed joins do not corrupt gameplay state.
Provide a recoverable staging area for invites, reconnects, loadouts, map votes, and region selection.
player_fantasy: Players feel they are entering a fair, organized session with the right friends, rules, and readiness before play starts.

parameters.name: lobby_id
parameters.description: Stable session staging identifier used by slot ownership, invites, reconnects, and match start.

parameters.name: matchmaking_ticket_id
parameters.description: Search request identifier used to cancel, retry, or audit matchmaking results.

parameters.name: lobby_slot_index
parameters.description: Authoritative seat assigned to a player or reserved party member inside the lobby.

parameters.name: ready_state
parameters.description: Per-player readiness state used by the start gate and lobby UI.

parameters.name: invite_code
parameters.typical_range: 6-12 character code or platform invite token
parameters.description: Join token that maps invited players to the correct lobby and privacy rule.

parameters.name: reconnect_token
parameters.description: Token that lets a disconnected player reclaim their reserved slot without creating a duplicate player record.

parameters.name: host_migration_state
parameters.description: State used when the lobby host leaves before a dedicated server or match instance is assigned.

edge_cases: The final player toggles ready while another player disconnects and the start request is already queued.
A party member joins through invite_code after the lobby filled their reserved slot with a solo player.
Host migration starts while matchmaking_ticket_id is being canceled or while map voting is unresolved.
A reconnecting client presents a valid reconnect_token but the match has already transitioned out of the lobby.
common_bugs: Two clients are assigned the same lobby_slot_index because join requests were accepted without an atomic slot reservation.
Match start reads stale ready_state data and launches with a player still changing loadout or team.
Leaving a party cancels the wrong matchmaking_ticket_id and strands remaining players in a dead lobby.
Late lobby updates arrive after gameplay loading and reopen the lobby UI over the match.
balancing_notes: Strict ready checks prevent accidental starts, but too many locks can make casual groups wait on inactive players.
Skill, region, platform, and ruleset filters should be visible enough that players understand longer search waits.
Host migration is valuable for party games but may be unnecessary if dedicated servers own every lobby transition.
accessibility_notes: Expose ready state, selected map, team, and queue status through text labels as well as icons.
Support voice-free coordination with ready prompts, invite links, and clear failure messages for reconnect or privacy errors.

implementation_notes.unity: With Netcode, NGO, or a service SDK, keep lobby_id, slot reservations, ready_state, and matchmaking_ticket_id in a lobby service layer before spawning gameplay NetworkObjects. Only the lobby authority should accept ready/start mutations, and reconnect_token should map back to an existing player_id rather than creating a second client object.
implementation_notes.godot: Model lobby state as a replicated data resource or authoritative server dictionary keyed by connection_id and lobby_slot_index. RPCs should request join, leave, ready, and start transitions; the server resolves them in order and broadcasts compact snapshots to Control UI.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Online Subsystem sessions or a custom backend for lobby metadata, with GameMode or a service owning start authority. Store ready_state, party_id, slot index, and host_migration_state outside transient widgets so seamless travel, reconnects, and failed travel can recover cleanly.
implementation_notes.web: For WebSocket or WebRTC lobbies, keep the lobby state on the authoritative server and send versioned snapshots to clients. Invite codes, reconnect tokens, and matchmaking tickets should expire server-side, and start requests should compare the expected lobby version before launching.

example_games.title: Rocket League
example_games.note: Uses lobbies and matchmaking queues before assigning players to online matches.

example_games.title: Among Us
example_games.note: Uses private room codes and pre-game lobby readiness for group sessions.
